claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents with cross-check evidence policy and blocks PRD readiness on boundary violations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 digests of all project files outside allowed_write_roots, using git ls-files when available or filesystem walk as fallback, with symlink detection.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation establishes baseline before agent execution.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added/modified/deleted files, returning sorted changes with path and change type.
evidence.why: Boundary violation detection logic identifies any project mutations after agents run.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots to the dispatch-specific output directory only.
evidence.why: Policy-level declaration that target project modification is forbidden during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution, detects changes, sets project_mutation status, and creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for violations.
evidence.why: Actual enforcement loop that audits agent behavior and blocks readiness on mutations.

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers are added to readiness for each detected change outside allowed_write_roots, keeping prd_ready=false until resolved.
evidence.why: Direct integration between boundary audit and PRD readiness gate.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with objectives, prompts, writes_code=false, and result_schema.
evidence.why: Formal protocol specification for evidence agents used in dispatch.

evidence.summary: hasTechnicalRiskSignal() detects field/schema/api/auth/state/dataflow keywords; hasHighCostSignal() detects payment/security/compliance/medical/production risks.
evidence.why: Trigger detection logic that determines when cross-check evidence policy applies.
Policy assignment logic that requires cross_check for high-risk brownfield work.

evidence.summary: Readiness check requires evidence agent results for all required roles (explorer/cross-checker/verifier) before prd_ready=true, adding EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers if missing.
evidence.why: PRD readiness gate enforcement that blocks completion without evidence.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates agent write outside allowed roots and verifies blocked status, violated boundary, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms boundary audit enforcement works as designed.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' and 'gitignored project mutations' verify boundary detects writes via git ls-files and gitignore-aware filesystem walk.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms boundary works with both git and non-git repos.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs Claude/codex/custom provider commands with -p flag, permission-mode, settings, tools, and budget controls.
evidence.why: Provider abstraction that evidence dispatch uses to spawn full-tool agents.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch uses prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots and readiness blockers as safety measures, not tool restriction.
evidence.why: Design intent confirmation that boundary audit is the primary safety mechanism.

evidence.summary: 260+ dispatch artifact directories exist, indicating repeated evidence dispatch runs in this working tree.
evidence.why: Artifact evidence that evidence dispatch has been executed in production.

risks: If boundary snapshot has git/filesystem race conditions or permission errors, mutations could go undetected.
If allowed_write_roots path resolution is incorrect, legitimate artifact writes could be flagged as violations.
If agents write files then delete them before snapshot, boundary may not detect the mutation.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch implements boundary audit via buildBoundarySnapshot/diffBoundarySnapshots, enforces project_mutation='forbidden' policy, and integrates with PRD readiness. Cross-check policy triggers on technical/high-cost risks. Tests verify git/non-git boundary detection. Doctrine confirms design intent.
